question: A valid Medical Participant Questionnaire (or valid medical statement) is required:

options.b: Prior to any open water training
options.d: Any time SSI training is conducted

explanation: Per SSI General Training Standards, the medical questionnaire (or signed physician statement) must be on file before the student enters the water — this includes confined-water sessions, not just open water. Pre-certification is too late; pre-OW only is too narrow.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: As defined in the General Training Standards, every SSI Dive Professional must use:

options.b: A DSMB and surface emergency signaling device

explanation: GTS requires the SSI Dive Professional to dive with at least all the equipment students are required to use, plus a personal dive computer, plus a DSMB and a surface signalling device — so all three statements together describe the minimum kit.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: Each student must have the appropriate Digital Training Kit (if available):

options.a: Only if they are not sharing with another student
options.b: Only if the instructor requires them to
options.c: Only if they are older than 18 years
options.d: For every program they participate in

explanation: SSI uses a one-kit-per-student-per-program model. The Digital Training Kit is the official student material set — it ensures each student has direct access to current standards, manuals, video and review tools, regardless of what their instructor or training centre prefers.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: As defined by the General Training Standards, a certified assistant is:

options.c: Able to assist with in-water training and supervision

explanation: A 'certified assistant' under GTS must be: (1) an SSI Dive Guide / Divemaster or higher, (2) in current/active status with SSI (renewed Pro membership, current insurance), and (3) qualified to assist with in-water training and supervision. All three apply.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: The Candidate Water Fitness Evaluation includes:

options.b: A full diver rescue with a surface swim in under 8 minutes
options.d: A 750-meter surface swim with mask, fins and snorkel

explanation: Per SSI General Training Standards, the Candidate Water Fitness Evaluation requires a full diver rescue with a surface swim completed in under 8 minutes — a timed, real-load rescue/tow demonstration. The other options are wrong: 50 m underwater is OW-level, 20 min tread is non-standard (15 min is the SSI norm), 750 m mask-fins-snorkel doesn't match SSI's distance benchmarks.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: Required open water training dives must be completed:

options.a: Within 9 months of the last academic or in-water training session
options.b: Within 6 months of the last academic or in-water training session
options.c: Within 12 months of the last academic training session
options.d: Within 6 months of the last academic training session

explanation: Per SSI General Training Standards, required open water training dives must be completed within 6 months of the last academic OR in-water training session — the clock starts from whichever was most recent. After 6 months, the student needs a refresher before completing the OW dives.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: If it has been more than 12 months since the last in-water session, an entry-level student:

options.b: Must complete a Scuba Skills Update
options.c: Must do a complete academic review

explanation: After >12 months out of the water, an entry-level student does not need to repeat the whole course — they complete an SSI Scuba Skills Update (refresher) covering academic review, equipment check and confined-water skill practice before continuing or diving.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: The SSI Flexibility Rule:

options.a: Requires skills to be taught exactly as described in the instructor manuals
options.b: Allows professionals to develop their own teaching style
options.c: Allows professionals to reduce the number of training dives as necessary
options.d: Allows professionals to add skills not endorsed by SSI to their training prog

explanation: The Flexibility Rule lets the instructor adapt teaching style, sequence and presentation to the student's needs and the local environment — but it does NOT permit reducing required content, omitting performance requirements, or adding non-SSI skills as part of the certification.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: Prior to beginning any training, each student must complete the:
explanation: Before training begins, every student signs/completes: (1) the program-specific Completion Record, (2) the Medical Participant Questionnaire, and (3) the Responsible Diver Code (plus liability release). All three are mandatory paperwork — no in-water activity until all are on file.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: An SSI Professional must have access to:
explanation: An active SSI Professional must keep current copies (or digital access) of the latest student materials they teach from, the latest instructor manuals, AND any additional standards/materials referenced for the programs they conduct. Outdated materials = standards violation.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards

question: In the General Training Standards, the rationale for teaching students to pass their primary regulator is found:
explanation: The 'SSI In-Water Training Philosophy' section of GTS is where SSI explains the WHY behind specific skill choices — including why students are taught to hand off their primary regulator to a panicked OOA buddy and switch to their own alternate. The other sections cover scope (How To Use), administrative procedures, or duty-of-care obligations rather than skill rationale.
source: mySSI: ITC Instructor Pretest — Part 6 General Training Standards
